assumeRoleArn.description:
Optional. Specifying the ARN of a role will ensure that the
                     selected authentication provider is used to assume the role rather than the
                     credentials directly.

authType.description: Specify which AWS credentials chain to use.

defaultDatabase.description: Default database to use as the {{database}} macro in queries.

defaultMeasure.description: Default measure to use as the {{measure}} macro in queries. Depends on the selected database and table.

defaultRegion.description: Specify the region, such as for US West (Oregon) use ` us-west-2 ` as the region.

defaultTable.description: Default table to use as the {{table}} macro in queries. Depends on the selected database.

endpoint.description: Optionally, specify a custom endpoint for the service

externalId.description: If you are assuming a role in another account, that has been created with an external ID, specify the external ID here.

profile.description: Credentials profile name, as specified in ~/.aws/credentials, leave blank for default.

examples.description: The defaults a fresh datasource starts with: AWS SDK Default credentials. `defaultRegion` still needs to be set before queries will run; secureJsonData is empty because the default provider reads credentials from the AWS SDK chain.

accessAndSecretKey.description: IAM user credentials supplied directly via secureJsonData. Optionally include sessionToken for temporary STS credentials.

assumeRoleFromKeys.description: IAM user credentials that then assume a cross-account IAM role via STS. `externalId` is optional and only meaningful when `assumeRoleArn` is set.

awsSdkDefault.description: Uses the AWS SDK default credential chain (env vars, shared config, EC2/ECS/EKS metadata). No secrets are set; the placeholder accessKey is empty to signal that.

credentialsFile.description: Reads a named profile from ~/.aws/credentials on the Grafana host. Leave `profile` blank to pick the default profile.

grafanaAssumeRole.description: Grafana Cloud's temporary-credentials broker. Feature-gated on the awsDatasourcesTempCredentials toggle; the editor hides externalId/endpoint when this provider is selected because Grafana derives them itself. Timestream is in the ConnectionConfig allow-list (`DS_TYPES_THAT_SUPPORT_TEMP_CREDS`).

legacyArnAuthType.description: Datasources provisioned before the 'arn' value was renamed still store `authType: "arn"`. The backend (awsds.AuthType.UnmarshalJSON) maps this to the modern 'default' at load time.

withMacroDefaults.summary: AWS SDK Default with Timestream macro defaults
withMacroDefaults.description: Seeds the {{database}}, {{table}}, and {{measure}} query macros so dashboards do not need to specify them per panel. The macro defaults are optional at save-time and can be overridden per query.

workspaceIamRole.description: Uses the IAM role attached to the Grafana workload (EC2 instance profile / ECS task role / EKS IRSA). Editor label is 'Workspace IAM Role'; storage value is 'ec2_iam_role'.
